note: aced-case-judge simulates the agent and scores it in ONE context while holding the scenario's name and its inline rubric -- the answer key. Every @rubric scenario in every ACED suite is measured by a simulator that has already been told the correct call, so an impl-gate PASS is weaker evidence than a run that separates production from scoring. Not a defect of any one suite; a protocol defect of the shared case-judge.
evidence: the case-judge's own stated SCENARIO input includes the scenario name and the inline rubric, and its task is to simulate then score from that same input

note: The two existing Oracle @rubric scenario titles name the branch they grade, and the case-judge receives the title and simulates from it -- so each hands the simulator the single-branch answer it exists to withhold. Same leak this CR's new scenario was retitled to avoid; closing it on the siblings is a frozen-scenario edit (a re-open), out of scope here.
evidence: the sibling titles state their branch and its action; the new dual-branch scenario was deliberately titled for the situation instead

why: Additive @rubric scenario on the frozen sdd/ssa-lowering suite closing #222: the Oracle scenarios were unfailable by construction -- SKILL.md's Oracle lens hands a clean stale-vs-misaligned taxonomy with one worked example each, and every frozen Oracle scenario was a clean single-branch instantiation, so a memorizing agent shape-matches to full marks whatever nouns the Givens use. New scenario is dual-branch: one ask dissolved by a change shipped since filing but leaving a live remnant (reshape, on supersession), one ask superseded by nothing and failing on direction-fit (kill, on misalignment). Both branches fire over different parts; the doctrine warns against conflating them but models no case that is both, so no template matches. Apparatus is music engraving -- swap-test clean, no absorption from the artifact's own examples (#220). Structural diff addOnly:true (added 1, modified 0, removed 0, unchanged 19) -> additive, self-clears, suite stays @frozen, no re-open. DISCRIMINATION PROVEN BY ABLATION with production SEPARATED from scoring (each plan produced by an agent blind to the rubric; each scored by a cold case-judge seeing one anonymized plan): reasoning read 6/6 PASS; memorizer firing the stale template wholesale 3/6 FAIL; memorizer firing misaligned wholesale 3/6 FAIL; mutant deleting the Misaligned branch 4/6 FAIL; mutant deleting the Stale branch 6/6 NO PENETRATION. The two memorizers land (3,0) and (0,3) -- each dimension observed at both full marks and zero, so independence is measured not asserted and neither dimension is free points. The #221 trap was hunted deliberately: spacing scored 3/3 across the first four conditions (the unfailable signature), so a fifth condition was built to kill it and it died at 0. First two mutants were DUDS -- the deleted branch's vocabulary survived elsewhere (the decision-evidence section names both branches) so the reads re-derived it; rebuilt scrubbed before they measured anything. Stale-mutant non-penetration REPORTED not papered over: spacing binds redundantly via 'When to run' (never trust the filing-time verdict); README states do not cite this scenario as Stale's guard. Owner accepts as a known scope edge -- this probe guards the dual-branch/misaligned discrimination, not stale-in-isolation. Scenario deliberately titled for the SITUATION not the verdict: the case-judge's input includes the scenario name and it simulates from it, so a title naming the per-part branch mapping is a live answer key; a local exception to the suite's title convention, commented. Siblings still leak their branch -> #253 (retitling them edits frozen scenarios, out of scope). Instrument weaker than the measurement: the case-judge simulates AND scores in one context holding the rubric -> #252; README states plainly that an impl-gate PASS here is worth strictly less than the separated-scoring table, rather than banking it. Cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED true conditional on 2 README gaps + 1 open marker, all closed; a second cold judge confirmed the closes and caught the fix reproducing the very defect it had just failed (a fresh 'tracked separately' with no reference). pnpm verify 21/21. LOW blast, additive; leash auto-none -- ratified by unional DIRECTLY in-session, not on the 4ea603 relay: the relayed-ratification seam bars a spawned session writing a human-attributed gate line on a coordinator's 'the user approved', so the conductor stopped and asked the human on their own channel instead.

note: The ssa-lowering doctrine teaches direction-fit only through an example where the direction is DECLARED as a slogan. It carries no rule to INFER direction from how a product ships when nothing declares it -- which is exactly what both misalignment probes withhold. Measured 2/3 on catches-misalignment at the #222 impl gate (scenario still passes at threshold). Matches the README's own note that this dimension measured 2.33 then 3.00 and does not replicate.
evidence: cold impl-judge scored the dimension 2/3 against the current doctrine; the README already records the non-replicating measurement

note: The doctrine's anti-over-merge rule lives in the placement step (new capabilities within one CR) and is never restated in the regroup step, where the over-merge temptation actually arises. Measured 2/3 on disjoint-nodes-not-fused at the #222 impl gate (scenario still passes at threshold).
evidence: cold impl-judge scored the dimension 2/3 and located the missing restatement in the regroup rule

note: The suite's boolean guards and its contention rubrics share near-verbatim vocabulary with the doctrine they grade, so full marks there confirm textual co-presence rather than independent derivation -- the same instrument-cannot-fail shape that got the cohesion guard demoted. #222 gives the suite its FIRST Oracle probe with a measured failing read; the other 19 scenarios remain untested against a memorizing subject. Standing measurement debt, not a defect of this CR.
evidence: two independent cold checkers flagged the shared vocabulary unprompted at the #222 impl gate; the cohesion guard carries the same finding

why: Cold aced-impl-judge ran the frozen ssa-lowering.feature: IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, 20/20 (1 @trigger outline 8/8 rows, 8 boolean guards, 11 @rubric incl. the new probe). pnpm verify 21/21. No SKILL.md edit shipped -- the CR's deliverable is the scenario and the current doctrine already carries it by reasoning, exactly as the pre-gate ablation predicted. THE JUDGE DEFEATED #252 RATHER THAN ENDURING IT: briefed that it could mark the probe CONTAMINATED if it could not separate the roles, it instead refused aced-case-judge for the new scenario and built the split itself -- 3 producers given the SUBJECT plus Givens/When ONLY (no title, no rubric, no Then, no siblings, no knowledge of being graded), each persisting its plan; then 3 cold scorers each reading one plan plus the rubric, blind to the SUBJECT and to each other. Production provably blind to the answer key. New probe 6/6, 6/6, 6/6 -> aggregate 6.0 >= 5, zero variance; all three scorers reached (3,3) by the CORRECT route (supersession for the spacing part, direction-fit for the cursor part, each explicitly refusing the other label). It audited the conductor's prior rather than aiming at it. The 19 pre-existing scenarios ran single-context and were reported as regression checks, not fresh evidence. ORTHOGONAL READ CAUGHT A REAL OVERCLAIM IN THE CONDUCTOR'S OWN PROSE: the README bound its 'an impl-gate PASS is weaker evidence than the table' caveat to the case-judge contamination -- a cause this very gate falsified by separating the roles. Left as written, a future pass that separates the roles would read the paragraph as discharged and equate its green gate with the table. Rewritten to bind to the structural axis: an impl gate runs the correct-doctrine arm ONLY; the two memorizers and two mutants are what prove the dimensions loseable and rule-bound, and a passing run never executes them -- a green gate says the doctrine still reasons, only an ablation says the probe can still fail. Contamination demoted to a per-run hazard (#252/#253). That prose fix landed AFTER the gate run: spec README only, not the subject and not the suite, so 20/20 stands; disclosed rather than left to be found. Impl gate clean (no reject -> no correction line for it). Three follow-ups recorded and filed: direction-fit taught only from a declared slogan with no infer-from-shipping rule (2/3), anti-over-merge never restated at the regroup step where the temptation arises (2/3), and -- the structural one -- the guards and contention rubrics share near-verbatim vocabulary with the doctrine they grade, so full marks confirm co-presence not derivation (same shape as the demoted cohesion guard, #250); 19 of 20 scenarios remain untested against a memorizing subject. Both 2/3 dimensions still pass at threshold and were NOT patched: editing SKILL.md to score better on probes just frozen is the contamination the owner ruled out. LOW blast, additive; leash auto-none -- ratified by unional DIRECTLY in-session on their own channel, not on the 4ea603 relay (the relayed-ratification seam bars a spawned session writing a human-attributed gate line on a coordinator's 'the user approved'). Closes #222.
